description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.

description: maritime branch of the United States military
description_wiki: The United States Navy (USN) is the maritime service branch of the United States Armed Forces and is designated as the navy of the United States in the Constitution.

description: bridge in Vermont to Chesterfield, New Hampshire

surface_forms: engineering aide for surveying and drafting
description: a person who assists in engineering work (valid in USA)
description_wiki: Engineering aide (abbreviated as EA) is a  United States Navy occupational rating.
Da Nang (Vietnamese: Đà Nẵng, pronounced [ʔɗaː˨˩ naŋ˨˩˦]), also spelt Danang, is the fourth largest municipality in Vietnam by population and the largest by geographical area.
The People's Army of Vietnam (PAVN) is the national military force of the Socialist Republic of Vietnam and the armed wing of the ruling Communist Party of Vietnam (CPV).

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Alabama ( , AL-ə-BAM-ə) is a state in the Southeastern and Deep Southern regions of the United States.

description: seat of San Diego County, California, United States; second-largest city in California
description_wiki: San Diego (  SAN dee-AY-goh; Spanish: [san ˈdjeɣo]) is a city on the Pacific coast of Southern California, adjacent to the Mexico–United States border.

aliases: State of Rhode Island and Providence Plantations
description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Rhode Island (  ROHD) is a state in the New England region of the Northeastern United States.

aliases: United States Naval Station Guantanamo Bay
description: military base of the United States Navy
description_wiki: Guantanamo Bay Naval Base (Spanish: Base Naval de la Bahía de Guantánamo), officially known as Naval Station Guantanamo Bay or NSGB, (also called GTMO, pronounced Gitmo  GIT-moh as jargon by the U.S. military) is a United States military base located on 45 square miles (117 km2) of land and water on the shore of Guantánamo Bay at the southeastern end of Cuba.

description: city in Ventura County, California, United States
description_wiki: Port Hueneme (  wy-NEE-mee; Chumash: Wene Me) is a small beach city in Ventura County, California, surrounded by the city of Oxnard and the Santa Barbara Channel.

description: US Marine Corps base near Oceanside, California, United States
description_wiki: Marine Corps Base Camp Pendleton is the major West Coast base of the United States Marine Corps and is one of the largest Marine Corps bases in the United States.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

aliases: Act to provide Federal Government aid for the readjustment in civilian life of returning World War II veterans
description: United States law that provided a range of benefits for returning World War II veterans
description_wiki: A G.I. Bill is a colloquial name given to several pieces of legislation that have provided a range of benefits for American military veterans, particularly education benefits.
